tool_args.label: Long Island Rail Road (LIRR)
tool_args.raw_text: Long Island Rail Road is comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

payload.description: Long Island Rail Road is comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

tool_args.label: Metro-North Railroad
tool_args.raw_text: Metro-North Railroad is comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

payload.description: Metro-North Railroad is comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

tool_args.label: Park Avenue Viaduct Replacement Project
tool_args.raw_text: Phase 1 of the Park Avenue Viaduct Replacement Project is now complete. Through 128 bridge installations, the project replaced 8,240 track feet of the aging 132-year-old structure, all without disrupting Metro-North service. Thanks to strong project management and an innovative construction approach, the project as a whole is $93 million under budget and 51 months ahead of schedule. Phase 2 is now underway.

payload.description: Phase 1 replaced 8,240 track feet of the aging 132-year-old structure through 128 bridge installations, all without disrupting Metro-North service. The project is $93 million under budget and 51 months ahead of schedule.

tool_args.label: Fleetwood Parking Lot Repair Project
tool_args.raw_text: This month, Construction & Development is advancing the Fleetwood Parking Lot Repair Project, which aims to bring the Fleetwood Station in Mount Vernon, New York to a state of good repair. A third-party firm contracted under the MTA's Small Business Mentoring Program (SBMP) is performing the construction. An over $1 million SBMP construction contract was awarded in July 2025, and the contract is performing the work in six phases to accommodate active parking operations at the lot. Phase one is currently underway.

payload.location: Fleetwood Station, Mount Vernon, New York
payload.description: Aims to bring the Fleetwood Station parking lot to a state of good repair. An over $1 million SBMP construction contract was awarded in July 2025, performing work in six phases to accommodate active parking operations.

location_normalized.raw_text: Fleetwood Station, Mount Vernon, New York

tool_args.label: Babylon Station Platform Replacement and Modernization
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development continues construction at LIRR Babylon Station to replace platforms and modernize the station. Built atop an elevated viaduct in Suffolk County, Babylon Station serves 5,000 riders daily. The project includes a complete replacement of two center-island 12-car platforms, along with upgrades to station amenities, accessibility improvements, and infrastructure upgrades.

payload.project_name: Babylon Station Platform Replacement and Modernization
payload.description: Complete replacement of two center-island 12-car platforms, along with upgrades to station amenities, accessibility improvements, and infrastructure upgrades. Phase 1 completed and opened for customers on May 19.

tool_args.label: Webster Avenue Bridge Replacement
tool_args.raw_text: Working closely with our partners at MTA Construction & Development, the 128-year-old Webster Avenue Bridge in Manhasset was replaced. The original structure dated back to 1897 and was in need of renewal. Now in its place is a modern, climate resilient bridge featuring new retaining walls and drainage improvements, a new roadway and sidewalks. And the best part – the project was completed on budget and four months ahead of schedule.

payload.status: completed on budget and four months ahead of schedule
payload.description: Replaced the 128-year-old Webster Avenue Bridge in Manhasset with a modern, climate resilient bridge featuring new retaining walls and drainage improvements, a new roadway and sidewalks.

tool_args.label: Park Avenue Viaduct Phase 1 Structural Completion
tool_args.raw_text: On Monday, October 6, I joined MTA Chair Janno Lieber and MTA Construction and Development President Jamie Torres-Springer to mark the structural completion of Phase 1 of the Park Avenue Viaduct Replacement Project.

payload.event_name: Park Avenue Viaduct Phase 1 Structural Completion
payload.description: MTA Chair Janno Lieber and MTA Construction and Development President Jamie Torres-Springer marked the structural completion of Phase 1 of the Park Avenue Viaduct Replacement Project.

tool_args.label: Super Express Service Launch on Hudson Line
tool_args.raw_text: In September, Governor Hochul today announced the launch of faster 'super-express' trains on the Hudson Line between Poughkeepsie and New York City with the upcoming Metro-North schedule effective Sunday, October 5. These new trips cut travel times between Poughkeepsie and Grand Central Terminal to less than 90 minutes each way.

payload.description: Launch of faster super-express trains on the Hudson Line between Poughkeepsie and New York City, cutting travel times to less than 90 minutes each way. Five daily trips between Poughkeepsie and Grand Central are now shortened to less than 90 minutes.

tool_args.label: Metro-North New Haven Open House
tool_args.raw_text: We held our annual Metro-North Open House in New Haven on Saturday, October 4, for the first time since 1990. Nearly 2,600 visitors filled the sparkling, spacious New Haven Component Change-Out Shop, their curiosity sparking with every exhibit.

payload.event_name: Metro-North Open House in New Haven
payload.description: Annual Metro-North Open House in New Haven, first time since 1990. Nearly 2,600 visitors attended at the New Haven Component Change-Out Shop.

tool_args.label: Dear New York Exhibition at Grand Central Terminal
tool_args.raw_text: For the first time in memory, Grand Central Terminal and the adjacent subway stations were cleared of advertising and replaced with art. Between October 6-19, more than 150 digital screens were unified to showcase thousands of portraits and stories from artist Brandon Stanton's Humans of New York archive

payload.event_name: Dear New York / Humans of New York Installation
payload.description: For two weeks in October, a Humans of New York installation took over Grand Central Terminal. More than 150 digital screens were unified to showcase thousands of portraits and stories from artist Brandon Stanton's Humans of New York archive.

tool_args.label: Signal Cutover at Divide Interlocking in Hicksville
tool_args.raw_text: As part of our constant work to modernize our core systems and keep our system in a state-of-good-repair, we are updating our signal system at Divide Interlocking in Hicksville. We will perform this signal upgrade on the weekend of November 8th and 9th, and it will require a shutdown of train service through the Hicksville area.

payload.event_name: Planned Signal Cutover Work in Hicksville
payload.description: Signal system upgrade at Divide Interlocking in Hicksville. Will require a shutdown of train service through the Hicksville area. Bus service will be provided and stations from Farmingdale to Ronkonkoma will have trains every two hours rerouted via the South Shore.

tool_args.label: Metro-North Extension to Albany
tool_args.raw_text: Governor Hochul announced last week that Metro-North will extend service to Albany in Spring 2026 to restore some frequency and capacity in the vital New York-Albany Empire Corridor.

payload.description: Metro-North will extend service north of Poughkeepsie for the first time, operating one weekday roundtrip extending an existing Grand Central-Poughkeepsie train with Metro-North locomotives and coaches, operated by Metro-North crews.

tool_args.label: LIRR new timetable November 2025
tool_args.raw_text: A new timetable will go into effect on November 10 and ending March 22. The new schedule includes an additional afternoon peak train to relieve crowding and support service adjustments related to track work on the Port Washington and Ronkonkoma branches.

tool_args.label: Help Point Installations on Ronkonkoma Branch
tool_args.raw_text: LIRR's Program Support team proudly hosted members of the LIRR ADA Task Force and the Commuter Council for a field visit on Wednesday, October 8. The day began at Deer Park Station, where Help Points have already been installed. From there, the group boarded a westbound train for a short ride to Wyandanch Station, where site assessments were conducted for upcoming installations. The final stop was Farmingdale Station, offering yet another opportunity to review proposed installation areas.

payload.description: Help Point units installed at Deer Park Station with site assessments conducted for upcoming installations at Wyandanch Station and Farmingdale Station on the Ronkonkoma Branch.

tool_args.label: Metro-North TRACKS virtual reality safety program
tool_args.raw_text: Metro-North has introduced new virtual reality headsets designed to look like our trains and mascot, TRACKS. The headsets bring safety education to life by letting people experience real railroad situations in a safe, controlled environment. They're part of our TRACKS outreach program, which teaches how to stay safe around trains and tracks. Now a popular program, it is expanding to reach first responders and commercial drivers who regularly work near the railroad

payload.claim_text: Metro-North has introduced new virtual reality headsets designed to look like our trains and mascot, TRACKS. The headsets bring safety education to life by letting people experience real railroad situations in a safe, controlled environment. The TRACKS outreach program is expanding to reach first responders and commercial drivers.
